edit_summary: Round-1 Reviewer B's three Major findings are resolved as follows. AMBIGUITY (a): the human-copy application timing is now pinned in Roles and Permissions, Main Workflows T-003, AC-011, and Edge Cases — the maintainer applies the staged .github/workflows/test.yml candidate as a commit on the feature PR branch before merge, so AC-009's live-file self-check goes green in the PR's own CI, and the pre-application red CI is documented as the designed fail-closed gate with no staged-candidate fallback (design.md Test Strategy item 6 was rewritten to remove the contradicting fallback wording, with matching updates in Deployment/CI Plan, Constraint Compliance, security-spec B3, and infra-spec CI/CD Sequence). AMBIGUITY (b): the dedup title marker is now a pinned literal ([model-freshness-divergence], Field Definitions) shared by both filing paths — REQ-001/AC-002 require D1's manual issues to carry it and the checklist to state it verbatim, and design.md's D1 checklist text and file_or_dedupe_issue sketch cite the identical string. EDGE-CASE-COVERAGE: new AC-020/TEST-020 lock the no-diff branch (fetch success, no divergence yields zero stubbed-gh invocations, exit 0), AC-009 now maps each of its four named branches to an explicit TEST ID, and the secondary issue-body gap is closed by new AC-021/TEST-021 asserting only charset-allowlist-validated model-ID tokens reach an issue body, with Security Boundary B1 extended to cover issue bodies in requirements, design, and security-spec. DOWNSTREAM-READINESS follows from the above: both previously-inventable behaviors are now explicit, traceable spec decisions; AC/TEST remain 1:1 at 21 pairs with no renumbering of existing IDs.
